subject: Ventral Hernias
title: Ventral Hernias: Classify and Don't Miss
hook: Get the classification right and the dangerous one never slips past you. Swipe.

points.heading: Incisional: the post-op hernia
points.icon: a surgical scar with a bulge
bullets: Common ventral hernia after abdominal surgery, especially laparotomy.
Wound infection is the single biggest risk factor.
Far rarer after laparoscopic port sites.

points.heading: Epigastric vs paraumbilical
points.icon: an abdominal midline with two bulges
bullets: Epigastric: linea alba between xiphoid and umbilicus.
Paraumbilical: narrow neck beside umbilicus, strangulates in adults.

points.heading: Richter's hernia is the silent one
points.icon: a loop of bowel with a pinched wall
bullets: Only the antimesenteric bowel wall gets trapped.
Can strangulate and perforate without complete obstruction.
That silent danger is exactly why it's tested.

points.heading: Spigelian: the lateral landmark
points.icon: an abdomen with a lateral defect line
bullets: Sits along the linea semilunaris, most below the umbilicus.
Defect hides under an intact external oblique.
The landmark name is usually your answer.

captionLines: Get the classification right and the dangerous one never slips past you.
Four to know: incisional after surgery, paraumbilical that loves to strangulate, the silent Richter's and the Spigelian along the linea semilunaris.
Master the map and the landmarks and these become free marks.
Recall Engine turns any lecture into SBAs, MCQs, flashcards and high-yield notes so you can drill these exact hernia facts until they stick.
